title: Effect inference within modules, required at pub boundaries — defend this
description: Private functions have inferred effects; pub functions require explicit effect annotations. A reviewer may suggest 'require effects everywhere for consistency' or 'infer effects everywhere including pub'. Defend: requiring effects everywhere is Rust's mistake — annotation burden everywhere. Inferring at pub boundaries violates the 'public API is the contract' principle — a caller cannot know what a pub function does without annotation. The current rule is the correct tradeoff: private = light, pub = explicit contract. This should be preserved.
